initial_failure.root_symptom: Graph-CoLD collapsed under CICIDS symmetric_20 while CoLD remained high.

root_causes: CICIDS is a flow-only dataset; process and threat_intel views must be inactive and excluded from mean fusion/CDM participation.
The tree-based smoke approximation combined soft sample weights with sklearn class_weight='balanced', which re-balanced on weighted class counts and distorted the intended weighted-loss semantics.
The clean smoke CDM surrogate incorrectly marked clean boundary samples in the no-noise case; clean data now keeps all samples below the deletion threshold.
Hard deletion was represented as zero sample weights under class_weight='balanced'; it is now implemented by filtering retained samples before fitting.
fixes_applied: Added expected_view_support metadata and active/inactive view filtering for CICIDS/MALTLS flow datasets.
Restricted CICIDS active views to host, ip, temporal; process and threat_intel are inactive.
Kept weighted CE normalization as sum(CE*w)/sum(w) and added tests.
Changed constant evidence min-max normalization to ones.
Used a shared y_noisy and flip_mask protocol across CoLD, ablation_hard, and Graph-CoLD smoke paths.
For tree smoke only, applied the ERR retention threshold tau_ret=0.1 before fitting sample-weighted ExtraTrees.
Replaced weighted class_weight interaction with explicit finite class-balance sample weights.

blocking_reasons: Full D5 matrix was intentionally not run for this smoke repair task.
Full D5 matrix was intentionally not run for this smoke repair task.
commands: python -m src.diagnostics.cicids_smoke_diagnosis --dataset cicids2017 --noise symmetric_20 --seed 42 --configs configs --out reports
python -m src.experiments.smoke_ablation --dataset cicids2017 --noise symmetric_20 --seed 42 --configs configs --out reports

python -m pytest -q tests/test_active_view_mask.py tests/test_weighted_loss_normalization.py tests/test_noisy_label_protocol.py tests/test_smoke_ablation_gate.py tests/test_cicids_smoke_diagnosis.py tests/test_err.py tests/test_smoke_realdata_gate.py
